item.title: Federal budget to produce $64 billion in savings | SBS News in Easy English 8 May 2026
item.description: A news bulletin for those learning English and those with disability

item.title: 'Race to the bottom': Donald Trump, the midterm elections, and a battle over maps
item.description: Battle lines have been drawn in anticipation of the United States midterm elections — quite literally.

item.title: Five key takeaways from the first week of antisemitism royal commission hearings
item.description: As the antisemitism royal commission wraps up its first week of hearings, here is what the inquiry has heard so far.

item.title: 'Stressed and worried': Uneasy feelings after IS-linked cohort returns
item.description: People hoping to leave behind traumatic memories are battling with the news of the arrivals from Syria.

item.title: A $100 billion Australian sector is struggling. For Angela, it means ‘working twice as hard’
item.description: Soaring costs are hurting Australia’s 2.7 million small businesses – especially in hospitality. So, what can the federal government’s May budget offer?

item.title: INTERVIEW: Professor Jessica Rosenberg on encouraging young people to engage on quantum technologies
item.description: How should Australia be preparing students for new technologies? It’s an issue governments and educators are grappling with across the world. American expert in the field, Professor Jessica Rosenberg from George Mason University, has been developing a school curriculum to encourage more young people to engage on quantum technologies. She’s also keen to see more girls and women in the field. During her visit to Australia she sat down with Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson to discuss the quantum task ahead.

item.title: Russia and Ukraine agree to renewed three-day ceasefire and prisoner swap, Trump says
item.description: The US president said the halt in fighting for Victory Day celebrations could mark the "beginning of the end" of the war.

item.title: A $100 billion Australian sector is struggling. For Angela, it means 'working twice as hard'
item.description: As costs soar for small businesses, tax relief and reduced red tape are tipped as support delivered in the federal budget.

item.title: Iran accuses US of 'reckless military adventure' after empty oil tankers struck
item.description: The US military claims it struck the ships in the Strait of Hormuz after they tried to breach its blockade on Iranian ports.

item.title: How do Australians really feel about each other? New data paints an 'alarming' picture
item.description: Widespread declines were observed in a survey exploring how much Australians trust each other and the country's institutions.

item.title: One Nation claims historic win in Farrer in poll seen as litmus test for Coalition
item.description: Voters in the southern New South Wales seat of Farrer have headed to the polls to choose a new MP after their former representative Sussan Ley resigned from parliament. Analysts say the vote could have lasting consequences for the coalition.

item.title: Eleven million people, just 500 doctors. How Australia's neighbour manages healthcare
item.description: It's Australia's closest neighbour and biggest aid recipient. But PNG's healthcare crisis has major impacts on patients and staff.

item.title: Maria has 13 children. It has its challenges, but there's one thing she's sure of
item.description: Maria El Khoury's children describe her as a strong role model who is patient, selfless and caring.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Health Minister Mark Butler ahead of the Budget
item.description: There are big changes ahead for the NDIS with some budget cuts and tightened eligibility. Health Minister Mark Butler says the government aims to remove around 160,000 participants by the end of the decade. Children with autism are the largest cohort of NDIS participants, with many set to be moved into the government's new thriving kids program and he says future eligibility will be based on a different set of criteria. He says work is also being done on cultural safety before Indigenous participants are transitioned off the NDIS under the new reform measures. Mr Butler's been talking the SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS News speaks to Treasurer Jim Chalmers ahead of the federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers talks One Nation, housing affordability and the economy with SBS World News Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson

item.title: Could One Nation's Farrer victory be the 'beginning of the end' for the Coalition?
item.description: The Farrer by-election delivered a historic result that could represent an "existential situation" for the Coalition.

item.title: Australia's dying middle class — and the rise of an inherited wealth nation
item.description: The wealth of Australia's middle-class has flatlined over five years but there's one group whose assets have soared.

item.title: Albanese vowed no changes to housing tax breaks. Now he's defending reforms
item.description: The federal government is making the case for controversial reforms to housing tax policies.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 11 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 11 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Why CSL sank, and what the Federal Budget could mean for investors
item.description: The ASX 200 slipped 0.5 per cent ahead of the Federal Budget as oil prices surged and investors weighed the prospect of tax reform. Banks were weaker across the board, while CSL plunged 16 per cent after cutting earnings expectations and flagging a major impairment.

item.title: What time is the 2026 federal budget? Here's everything we know so far
item.description: Here are some of the big-ticket items that will impact Australians.

item.title: Scrolling through his phone after his marriage ended, Tim realised he had no one to call
item.description: For some men, a lack of emotional resources means profound struggle and suicidal ideation can follow breakups.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 12 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Town 'at the end of the world' denies hantavirus origins as travellers warned
item.description: Authorities have pointed the finger at an Argentine tourism hotspot, but locals have accused the government of a "smear campaign".

item.title: BHP reclaims crown as Australia's largest listed company
item.description: BHP surged to a record high and overtook Commonwealth Bank as Australia’s largest listed company, helping lift the materials sector evenas the ASX200 fell for a third straight session. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Jonathan Shead from State Street Investment Management unpack BHP’s milestone, the strength in mining stocks and the broader investment environment. NAB Head of Australian Economics Gareth Spence also joins, to explain why business conditions have dropped to their weakest level since 2020 and how the conflict in the Middle East is driving up costs across supply chains.

item.title: Where your tax money goes: Breaking down the federal budget as $100
item.description: It's a huge budget — we've broken it down into a simple $100 snapshot to show where every dollar goes.

item.title: Who are the winners and losers of the 2026 federal budget?
item.description: Small business owners, first home buyers, migrants and travellers are just some of the Australians impacted by the 2026-27 federal budget.

item.title: Your five-minute guide to the budget: Key changes Australians should know
item.description: The key measures, major announcements and biggest takeaways from the federal budget — explained simply.

item.title: 2026-27 federal budget migration numbers: What's changing and who's affected
item.description: The budget has confirmed the number of permanent migrants to be allowed into Australia.

item.title: Treasurer cites 'hard road of reform' as he reveals 2026 federal budget
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has delivered his 2026 budget, which he says takes what he's called the 'hard road of reform'. At the heart of the budget are housing tax changes, and a $35 billion saving from reform to the runaway National Disability Insurance Scheme. Labor has been keen to portray this budget as one of spending restraint, after warnings by economists and Reserve Bank governor Michele Bullock that splashing out would only fuel inflation and risk further rate hikes.

item.title: Treasurer flags 'fairer' budget as business groups criticise tax reforms
item.description: The Federal Budget has been handed down, including major tax changes that have been welcomed by unions, social services groups, and renters groups. Industry groups have raised concerns, saying the tax changes will be bad for business, as debate continues over how to balance fairness and fiscal restraint.

item.title: A drawing my son did at kindergarten made me quit my corporate job
item.description: Terry Murton says his obsession with climbing the corporate ladder stopped him from being the father and husband he wanted to be.

item.title: Why the budget might not 'shift the dial' on home ownership
item.description: The government says its housing tax reforms will help first home buyers — but some are doubtful.

item.title: 'Devoid of all military logic': Zelenskyy fumes as Russia targets Ukraine civilians
item.description: The attacks came following the end of a three-day ceasefire to mark the Soviet victory in World War Two.

item.title: Investors, renters react cautiously to Australia changing negative gearing, capital gains tax
item.description: Speculation was rife in the lead up to this year's budget about what it would contain for housing investors. Now we know what Treasurer Jim Chalmers has planned for the capital gains tax discount, and negative gearing regime, both of which have been blamed for favouring investors over first home buyers and inflating prices. What do the changes mean for renters and potential buyers in the housing market?

item.title: President Donald Trump set to arrive in Beijing SBS | News in Easy English 13 May 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Critics pay out on PM for 'cowardly' gambling reforms
item.description: The government has finally filed its formal response to the gambling inquiry chaired by the late MP Peta Murphy. But their response has drawn sharp criticism from advocates, who say it was dropped the day of the federal Budget - and that it doesn't go far enough.

item.title: We asked what you wanted to know about the budget. Here are the answers
item.description: SBS News asked audiences what mattered most to them in the federal budget. These were the key questions.

item.title: 'Devastated' disability community reacts to federal budget
item.description: Cuts to the National Disability Insurance Scheme are the largest source of savings in the federal budget, leaving many in the disability community angry and anxious about what's to come. The details of which participants will lose their supports, and whether state governments will fill those gaps, are still being worked out.

item.title: 'Grandfathering' vs new builds: The key housing terms and who will get a tax perk
item.description: While the changes privilege "people on the basis of birth order", they have been largely welcomed by economists.

item.title: On The Money: What Budget 2026 means for property, investments and the economy
item.description: The ASX closed lower, after a record 10.4 per cent slump in CommBank shares wiped around $25 billion from its market value, while investors digested major Federal Budget reforms targeting negative gearing and capital gains tax.

item.title: Budget 2026: SBS goes one-on-one with Prime Minister Anthony Albanese
item.description: SBS chief political correspondent Anna Henderson quizzes the Prime Minister about the budget, broken promises, a gas tax, and more

item.title: Indigenous Australia reacts to the 2026 federal budget
item.description: The Federal Government says it's investing $1.2 billion in First Nations communities and Closing the Gap over five years as part of Tuesday's budget. Headline measures include new funding for remote jobs, community controlled health infrastructure, and programs to combat gendered violence. Despite these investments, some community leaders have told NITV they feel the budget left First Nations people behind.

item.title: Australia v the world: How our spending compares globally
item.description: Budgets inevitably involve trade-offs, so how do Australia's priorities line up with the world?

item.title: The 13 'Down Down' tickets behind Coles' court loss — and what the ruling could mean
item.description: Justice Michael O'Bryan found Coles had misled consumers in a judgment handed down on Thursday morning.

item.title: Coles misled customers in pricing campaign | SBS News in Easy English 14 May 2026
item.description: A five minute bulletin for English language learners

item.title: Football chief seeks Iraqi citizenship for Australian coach Graham Arnold
item.description: Former Australian national coach Graham Arnold has become a hero in the Middle Eastern country.

item.title: Trump and Xi face-off, Starmer staggers on, while Macron has a word…
item.description: Taiwan on tenterhooks as they watch Donald Trump in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping. The Iran war enters its eleventh week with ceasefire talks faltering and leadership challenges plague the UK Prime Minister. Plus, Eurovision faces boycotts over Israel’s participation and France’s President, Emmanuel Macron, chides a Nairobi crowd.

item.title: Four audience members ejected from Eurovision over 'disruptive behaviour' during Israel set
item.description: Israel's contestant at the Eurovision Song Contest semi-final says he heard the boos of a small number of protesters as he ‌took the stage.

item.title: The Aussies who could be caught in the middle of the housing tax shake-up
item.description: Tax changes designed to help younger Australians into the housing market could also create unintended consequences, experts say.

item.title: Xero CEO on the SaaSpocolypse, AI integration and impact on the economy
item.description: The ASX200 closed slightly higher after a late-session rebound, with mining giants BHP and Rio Tinto hitting fresh records while Coles fell after a landmark ACCC court ruling over misleading discount pricing. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Betashares investment strategist Hugh Lam about the day’s market action and the forces driving investor sentiment. Plus, Xero CEO Sukhinder Singh Cassidy responds to concerns artificial intelligence could disrupt the software-as-a-service sector, outlining how the cloud accounting company plans to navigate the next phase of the AI revolution after reporting a sharp fall in profit.

item.title: Budget reply: From tax reform to migrant caps and welfare cuts, here are six takeaways
item.description: Capping Australia’s net overseas migration and tying it to housing supply are central pillars of the Coalition’s plan.

item.title: 'Pretty strange': Opposition's vision on housing and migration may not add up, experts say
item.description: The Opposition's pitch centres around cuts to migration and measures to boost housing supply. So how do they stack up?

item.title: Branko made a hard choice to sacrifice his citizenship. These Aussies hope to get theirs back
item.description: Thanks to a quirk of Dutch citizenship, thousands of Australians have been denied passports.

item.title: One Nation copy cat? Opposition accused of borrowing Hanson's policies in budget reply
item.description: Coalition leader Angus Taylor has delivered his first budget reply, vowing to crack down on migration levels and prohibit non-citizens from accessing welfare payments. Critics say the coalition is blaming migrants for Australia's economic troubles, as Pauline Hanson's anti-immigrant One Nation accuses the opposition of copying their policies.

item.title: Trump signals Chinese President "would love to help" in conflict with Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump says China has offered to help calm conflict between the US and Iran, after high-stakes talks with President Xi Jinping in Beijing that focused heavily on the war in the Middle East, the Strait of Hormuz and growing fears of global economic fallout. The visit also exposed the delicate balancing act facing both powers, with Taiwan, trade and military tensions still threatening to destabilise the relationship between Washington and Beijing.

item.title: Sam Kerr to leave Chelsea after six years and trophy-laden career with London club
item.description: The Matildas captain is expected to make a move to the US National Women's Soccer League team Denver Summit.

item.title: 'Become a citizen': Taylor questioned over welfare payment cuts for permanent migrants
item.description: The Opposition has faced questions about what constitutes an Australian and who will be cut off from welfare payments under his new proposal.

item.title: The Coalition's proposed migration changes slammed | SBS News in Easy English 15 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners

item.title: 'No place in Australia': Burke adds 'neo-Nazis' to proscribed hate group list
item.description: The home affairs minister has used powers established in the wake of the Bondi attack to prohibit a second hate group.

item.title: Could Australia win Eurovision? The odds are looking Goodrem
item.description: Delta Goodrem has progressed to the grand final of the Eurovision Song Contest, where she is one of the favourites to win.

item.title: How AI is rewriting resumes and the ASX ends tough week lower
item.description: On this episode of the SBS On the Money podcast, Stephanie Youssef speaks with Michael Page Senior Managing Director David George about the growing use of artificial intelligence in recruitment, with more job seekers and employers turning to AI to write resumes, job ads and interview questions. Plus, Ricardo Gonçalves is joined by Tribeca Investment Partners Portfolio Manager Todd Warren to unpack a volatile week on the Australian sharemarket, as profit taking in the mining sector helped drag the ASX 1.3% lower for the week.

item.title: The $3.1 trillion tax secret hiding in plain sight
item.description: Some Australians have benefited for decades from a secretive arrangement that allows them to hide assets and reduce tax.

item.title: The $3.1 trillion tax secret hiding in plain sight
item.description: Some Australians have benefited for decades from a secretive arrangement that allows them to hide assets and reduce tax.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Angus Taylor questioned by Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor says a Coalition government would remove 75,000 visa overstayers from Australia. In his budget reply, he also announced that non-citizens will be blocked from accessing 17 welfare programs. One Nation says Angus Taylor has copied its migration policy. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked Mr Taylor whether he's moving further to the right to try to court One Nation voters.

item.title: As Xi sets a new power dynamic, Trump leaves China with little to brag about
item.description: Throughout a largely cordial summit, there were signals that the balance of power between the two leaders might be shifting.

item.title: Hopes for 'lasting peace': Israel and Lebanon extend ceasefire by 45-days
item.description: A US official has described the talks between the countries as "highly productive".

item.title: How Australian housing became a generational budget battleground
item.description: Housing taxation changes have upset some investors. If the government can win the support of young people, that may not matter.

item.title: Next-generation Indigenous women are leading the way on employment growth
item.description: A new study has shown that employment rates for Indigenous men and women are now nearly equal.

item.title: Australia has a growing $56 billion problem - is this a solution?
item.description: As more women face mental health challenges, experts are pushing for more single-gender psychiatric wards.

item.title: INTERVIEW: Greens leader Larissa Waters responds to the Federal Budget
item.description: Greens leader Larissa Waters says the federal government should be setting out to build more public and affordable housing similar to the way previous governments used to build large-scale public housing estates. Senator Waters says housing affordability demands bold reform but says the federal government changes to property taxes in the Budget amounts to tinkering around the edges. SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson asked her whether the Greens will back the changes to negative gearing and capital gains.

item.title: Israel’s Eurovision voting campaign is in the spotlight — again. Here’s why
item.description: Israel's public broadcaster was formally warned by Eurovision last week

item.title: Gaza mosques announce Hamas military leader's death
item.description: Witnesses said mosques ⁠announced ‌Izz al-Din al-Haddad's "martyrdom" after Israel's military said ‌airstrikes had ‌targeted ⁠him.

item.title: These Greek islands are booked, busy, and built up. Now locals are calling for change
item.description: For Australians, Santorini and Mykonos are popular holiday destinations. But locals say their home is being 'destroyed'.

item.title: Prepper used to be a dirty word. But these 'ordinary' people think it's just sensible now
item.description: One lives in the bush for $50 a week. Another grows vegetables in South Australia. Meet Australia's doomsday preppers.

item.title: Antisemitism, Zionism and Israel: Royal commission hearings take a political turn
item.description: Differentiating antisemitism and criticism of Israel was one focal point of the commission's second week of hearings.

item.title: Tanya was struggling to cope with daily tasks. When she found out why, it was life-changing
item.description: One in four women in Australia faces mental health challenges. However, some are at risk when seeking help in mixed-gender hospital wards. An Australian psychiatrist is working to change that.

item.title: The Aussies who could be caught in the middle of the housing tax shake-up
item.description: Who are the Australians who could be caught in the middle of housing tax reforms in the federal budget? The government says changes to negative gearing and capital gains tax are designed to improve housing affordability and level the playing field for younger generations. But some experts warn the reforms could also create unintended consequences for Australians trying to build wealth and break into the property market.

item.title: Eurovision 2026: Bulgaria sweeps grand final as Australia's Delta Goodrem places fourth
item.description: The finals weren't without controversy, boos erupting from the audience as Israel's points totals were announced.

item.title: Who is DARA, the Bulgarian singer who won Eurovision 2026?
item.description: DARA's song Bangaranga swept voting at the famed song competition, beating out Israel, Romania and Australia.

item.title: Taylor vows to regain voter 'trust' but says prospect of One Nation in Coalition is 'nonsense'
item.description: As the Liberal Party loses voters to One Nation, Angus Taylor has played down the idea of expanding the Coalition to include it.

item.title: A major tax deadline is coming next year — could it spark a rush to sell?
item.description: The changes to CGT will take effect starting in July 2027, giving investors time to decide what to do with their properties.

item.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak an international health emergency
item.description: The outbreak of the highly contagious disease began in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda.

item.title: EV battery supply chains in focus as China halts sulphuric acid exports
item.description: China ceasing exports of sulphuric acid is expected to put pressure on both EV battery and fertiliser supply chains

item.title: Fans celebrate ‘sun goddess' Delta Goodrem’s Eurovision Grand Final performance
item.description: Delta Goodrem 'stoked' with Eurovision result, as Sydney fans celebrate her dazzling Grand Final performance

item.title: One Nation's popularity is rising. Which Australians are supporting the party and why?
item.description: Australians from across the political spectrum give their views on One Nation following the party's recent by-election victory.

item.title: Bigger than ever: What makes this year's FIFA World Cup unique
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will span three countries, feature 104 matches and introduce new measures to curb time-wasting.

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: 'Huge step forward': Ozempic endorsed to treat obesity and heart disease in Australian first
item.description: Two-thirds of Australians are at risk of cardiovasular disease. Could GLP-1 medications be the answer?

item.title: This charity receives millions from the government. Workers say conditions were 'like a jail'
item.description: Migrant workers contracted under the PALM scheme to labour hire company Madec Australia say they are being treated unfairly.

item.title: UAE reports drone strike at nuclear power plant as Trump says 'clock is ticking' for Iran
item.description: A drone strike has targeted the United Arab Emirates' sole nuclear power plant, sparking a fire, officials say.

item.title: Ukrainian drone strikes kill four in Russia in its biggest attack in over a year
item.description: Volodymyr Zelenskyy says Ukraine's increased attacks on Moscow have produced a "change in the balance of actions at the front".

item.title: What is Ebola and what makes the latest outbreak so concerning?
item.description: The WHO has declared an international health emergency in multiple African countries over the spread of the deadly disease.

item.title: US Iran ceasefire threatens to crumble as leaders prepare to meet
item.description: There are reports emerging that Israeli and US authorities are preparing to resume attacks on Iran. There are also concerns of regional neighbors being brought into the conflict, as the United Arab Emirates has reported a drone strike at a nuclear power plant.

item.title: Australia works with international countries to co-ordinate response to Ebola | SBS News in Easy English 18 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners

item.title: Jim Chalmers defends budget tax reforms following critical polls
item.description: A Resolve poll suggested Angus Taylor had overtaken Anthony Albanese as preferred prime minister for the first time.

item.title: Labor defends budget housing tax changes despite critical polls
item.description: The contest continues over the future of Australia's tax system following last week's Federal Budget. The Coalition has flagged its own bold policies on tax and housing, while the Treasurer is working to sell a trio of changes affecting negative gearing, capital gains and trusts. Two new opinion polls suggest his federal budget has been poorly received by voters - but Jim Chalmers has declared the long-term gains from controversial housing tax changes are worth the short-term political pain.

item.title: WHO declares global health emergency in Congo, Uganda outbreak of Ebola
item.description: The World Health Organisation has declared an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is a public health emergency of international concern. The WHO says it does not meet the criteria of a pandemic emergency, but that countries sharing land borders with the DRC are at high risk of further spread.

item.title: ASX slumps to seven week slow as global inflation fears escalate
item.description: The ASX200 has fallen to a seven-week low as surging oil prices and rising inflation fears rattle global markets. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with David Scutt from StoneX Group Inc. about the market sell-off, the impact of the Middle East crisis on energy prices, and why investors are now bracing for possible interest rate rises in the United States after inflation climbed to a three-year high. The episode also looks at sharp losses for Brambles and major miners including BHP.

item.title: Rainy days ahead for much of eastern Australia, as a dry winter looms
item.description: It's going to be a wet couple of days for Australians on the east coast, while the west will be dry and settled.

item.title: Why this migrant newspaper is still thriving — a century later
item.description: The Greek Herald is still keeping generations of Greek Australians connected to language, community and culture.

item.title: No gloves, no approval: Inside the fight club bringing bare knuckle boxing to backyards
item.description: For these men, the desire to fight in bare knuckle backyard bouts outweighs the risk of injury or even imprisonment.

item.title: 'Nightmare scenario': inquiry lays bare the impact of illegal tobacco trade
item.description: A coalition of health organisations says tobacco companies are exploiting concerns about the illicit cigarette trade to undo decades of smoking control policies and push for tax cuts. The health organisations say they are particularly concerned by reports MPs heard evidence from tobacco giant Philip Morris in secret.

item.title: Trump says he held off new attack on Iran after Gulf countries' request
item.description: Trump said the US military is still "prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault" on Iran if no deal is reached.

item.title: Cuba warns of 'bloodbath' amid Trump pressure: Is a US attack looming?
item.description: Relations between the two countries have deteriorated sharply in recent days. Experts say Trump's aims in the region are unclear.

item.title: Three people killed in San Diego mosque shooting, two suspected gunmen found dead
item.description: The mother of one of the suspects had called police about two hours before the shooting, San Diego police chief Scott Wahl said.

item.title: Police charge man with triple murder after woman and children found dead in Sydney home
item.description: Police found the bodies of two boys, aged four and 12, and a 46-year-old woman inside a home in Campbelltown, Sydney.

item.title: US holds off planned Iran attack as nations resume talks
item.description: United States President Donald Trump says he has held off on an attack on Iran that was planned for today after Gulf countries' request. But President Trump says the U-S military is still prepared to go forward with a full, large scale assault on Iran if no deal is reached.

item.title: Taylor responds after Liberal senator breaks ranks over Coalition's migration policy
item.description: The Coalition should be wary of its tone and not approach migration as a "negative", a Liberal senator has said.

item.title: Millions across Australia celebrating annual National Volunteer Week
item.description: Millions of volunteers across Australia are celebrating the annual National Volunteer Week this week, sharing their joy of contributing back to their own communities. The theme for the National Volunteer Week this year is 'Your Year to Volunteer', honouring the contributions of volunteers and calling for more to join volunteering.

item.title: 'Far-reaching impacts': Why there are fears over a pledge to amend discrimination laws
item.description: The Coalition has vowed to "define biological sex" in the Sex Discrimination Act as a first-term priority if it wins government.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 19 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability.

item.title: Families 'terrified' for Australians on Gaza flotilla detained by Israel
item.description: The wellbeing of 11 Australian activists is unknown after their Gaza-bound flotilla was intercepted overnight.

item.title: RBA warns of future business price hikes as ASX rebounds from seven week low
item.description: The Australian sharemarket bounced back from a seven-week low as easing tensions in the Middle East lifted investor confidence, with the ASX200 rising 1.2 per cent. Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Westpac Senior Economist Matthew Hassan about why Australians remain deeply pessimistic despite a slight lift in consumer sentiment, as the Reserve Bank warns businesses are preparing further price hikes and workers push for higher wages. Plus, Michael Jenneke from UBS Global Wealth Management breaks down the day’s market action, including gains for supermarkets, banks and telcos, while energy stocks slipped as oil prices eased.

item.title: This childhood disease nearly vanished. Now we're seeing its worst outbreak in decades
item.description: The vaccine that prevents this "ancient" disease is freely available. Experts say its resurgence is alarming.

item.title: Two dead from mosquito-borne virus as NT authorities urge precautions
item.description: NT Health has recommended people take preventative measures, including using insect repellent and covering up.

item.title: Middle Australia has been forgotten; that's why I support One Nation. My niece disagrees
item.description: Renee recently switched from voting for the Coalition to One Nation, while her niece Sarah plans on voting for a left-leaning party.

item.title: A dangerous communist label — and the Australian funding facing scrutiny
item.description: For those who are red-tagged in the Philippines, the label can carry dangerous consequences.

item.title: Collins-class submarines extended | Morning Bulletin 20 May 2026
item.description: The Government extends the life of Collins-Class submarines amidst the transition to nuclear subs; the US warns Iran more military strikes remain possible despite progress in talks; and in sport, big consequences in English football's spying scandal.

item.title: WHO 'deeply concerned about scale and speed' of deadly Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization says 26 more suspected Ebola deaths have been recorded in 24 hours.

item.title: Trump says Iran wants a peace deal but warns US may strike again
item.description: President Donald Trump warns the US may strike Iran again, a day after he claims he held off a major assault in hopes of a peace deal.

item.title: Compensation fund for Trump allies blasted as corrupt
item.description: US President Donald Trump's allies who believe they have been wrongly investigated and prosecuted could soon have access to a nearly $1.8 billion compensation fund. The US Justice Department has made the announcement in a move to settle a lawsuit against the US Tax Office, heavily criticised by Democratic Party members as unconstitutional and corrupt.

item.title: Another Ebola outbreak in Africa: SBS speaks to an expert on the ground
item.description: The World Health Organisation has declared an Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda is a public health emergency of international concern. SBS' Alexandra Jones speaks to the WHO's Dr Anne Ancia, who is in Congo DR, for an insight on the situation.

item.title: 'Our business partner, Anthony Albanese': The 47 per cent tax claim going viral after the budget
item.description: Startup founders and small business owners have spread AI-generated memes about proposed changes to the capital gains tax.

item.title: Sydney shooting leaves one person dead, four in hospital
item.description: Police have established a crime scene at a south-west Sydney home after five men self-presented to hospital on Tuesday night.

item.title: Race on to contain Ebola outbreak | Midday Bulletin 20 May 2026
item.description: Isolation of cases and contacts crucial as response to Ebola in Congo continues; Israel says its intercepted all 50 boats in an aid flotilla attempting to reach Gaza; and in football, more big European-based names join the Socceroos' pre-World Cup training camp.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 20 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: Families of Australian flotilla activists 'anxious' amid reports they're being held in Israel
item.description: There has still been no word from the 11 Australian activists intercepted while travelling on the Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

item.title: 'Very difficult for the world': Albanese sharpens criticism of Trump
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese said Donald Trump's ever-changing positions on the war were hurting economies, including Australia.

item.title: 'Why shouldn't she talk to me?': Pauline Hanson reveals policy talks with Gina Rinehart
item.description: Pauline Hanson has also spoken to SBS News about running Muslim One Nation candidates, neo-Nazi supporters and western Sydney voters.

item.title: Cases climb in Australian diphtheria outbreak | Evening News Bulletin 20 May 2026
item.description: Australia records the biggest diphtheria outbreak in decades; Lebanon says Israeli airstrikes kill 19 in the south; In basketball, the Opals to face China before the Women’s World Cup.

item.title: Second group of IS-linked women have no government support, Albanese says
item.description: The Opposition has criticised the government, saying they've not done enough to stop the IS-linked women from returning.

item.title: Diphtheria cases rise as WA sees outbreak for first time in 50 years
item.description: Australia’s worst diphtheria outbreak in decades has triggered vaccine warnings as cases rise in WA and NT.

item.title: Advocate's call for Royal Commission into femicide receives mixed response
item.description: A WARNING THAT THIS STORY MAY BE DISTRESSING FOR SOME LISTENERS. An alleged triple murder in south-west Sydney has prompted renewed calls for a Royal Commission into femicide. It comes amid ongoing strain on women's shelters and other frontline services, with calls for greater funding. The government has embraced the aim of ending gendered violence within a generation - but advocates say that won't be achieved without greater effort.

item.title: What proposed CGT changes mean for small business as ASX hits 7 week low
item.description: The ASX 200 has fallen to a fresh seven-week low, on fears of rising global interest rates. SBS Finance Editor Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Francesco De Stradis from Ord Minnett about the market sell-off and the key drivers behind it. Plus, William Buck Tax Services Partner Todd Want breaks down the federal government’s proposed tax reforms after online memes claiming the Prime Minister would “own” 47 per cent of small businesses sparked confusion and debate.

item.title: 'My dear friend': Putin and Xi praise partnership during talks in Beijing
item.description: While Russia faces economic and financial sanctions from other countries, China remains a prominent and loyal customer.

item.title: Why it's been hard to detect and contain the world's broadening Ebola crisis
item.description: A rare Ebola strain is spreading through the conflict-hit Democratic Republic of the Congo's east.

item.title: US charges former Cuban President Raul Castro with murder | Morning News Bulletin 21 May 2026
item.description: Former Cuban President Raul Castro indicted on murder charges in the US; Australia's diphtheria outbreak continues to grow; and in sport, South Sydney's Jai Arrow retires from the NRL after being diagnosed with Motor Neurone disease.

item.title: Ex-Cuban president Raúl Castro indicted on murder charges as Trump escalates pressure
item.description: The 94-year-old has been indicted over the deadly 1996 downing of two civilian planes.

item.title: The visa rule change affecting Australians going to this popular travel destination
item.description: Aussies looking to escape the winter cold overseas could be in for a surprise.

item.title: Iran examines US proposal as Donald Trump says negotiations are in 'final stage'
item.description: The US president said "we're going to do some things that are a little bit nasty" if Iran doesn't agree to the deal.

item.title: Israel envoy says Ben-Gvir not reflective of country, as activist treatment condemned
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin ⁠Netanyahu said security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of activists was wrong.

item.title: Israel envoy says Ben-Gvir not reflective of country, as activist treatment condemned
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin ⁠Netanyahu said security minister Itamar Ben-Gvir's treatment of activists was wrong.

item.title: Australian Muslims call for action as US authorities investigate mosque attack as hate crime
item.description: An investigation is underway into a deadly shooting at a mosque in the US city of San Diego, which police and the FBI are treating as a hate crime. The three men who died are also being praised for their courage in drawing the attackers away from the school that was next to the place of worship.

item.title: 'We don't need more reviews': Australia's femicide inquiry debate
item.description: An alleged domestic violence triple murder has prompted renewed calls for a royal commission into femicide.

item.title: Does an El Niño loom? Here's what winter could look like across Australia
item.description: Parts of Australia are seeing record rainfall, but the BoM says the drenching won't last.

item.title: Wong hauls in Israeli ambassador over 'shocking' flotilla video | Midday News Bulletin 21 May 2026
item.description: Foreign Minister Penny Wong labels berating of bound pro-Palestine activists "shocking and unacceptable"; Sons of man killed in US mosque attack say he's a hero; and in sport, Southampton's appeal on spying charge fails.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS talks to One Nation leader Pauline Hanson
item.description: Pauline Hanson has spoken with SBS Chief Political Correspondent Anna Henderson in a wide-ranging interview following the federal government’s 2026/27 budget announcement.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 21 May 2026
item.description: A daily five minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability. Listen to Australian and world news, and follow trending topics with SBS News Podcasts.

item.title: Coalition positions itself as small business champion after Labor's budget tax reforms
item.description: The Coalition has vowed to support the small business sector as consternation grows over tax changes affecting capital gains. In his budget reply, Shadow Treasurer Tim Wilson accused Labor of an attack on aspiration, and laid out the Liberal vision.

item.title: Drop in female employment drives jobless rate increase | Evening News Bulletin 21 May 2026
item.description: In this bulletin, Unemployment climbs to 4.5 per cent; The federal government defends its response to the diphtheria outbreak; Jobs on the line at Football Australia.

item.title: Australia endorses historic UN resolution for climate change accountability
item.description: Australia voted in favour of endorsing the advisory opinion despite being one of the world's leading exporters of coal and gas.

item.title: 'Great triangle reversed': Xi met Trump, then Putin. It showed the world could be shifting
item.description: One of the Chinese president's meetings centred on deals. The other focused on confrontation.

item.title: Why measles, diphtheria and whooping cough cases are rising in Australia
item.description: These diseases were considered rare in Australia, having no recorded outbreaks for decades — until now.

item.title: Israel's ambassador to Australia summoned over 'shocking' flotilla video
item.description: A Gaza-bound aid flotilla has not reached its destination. Instead, footage of activists aboard it being forced to kneel on the concrete at Israel’s Ashdod port, surrounded by armed officers and taunted by far-right minister Itamar Ben-Gvir, has triggered international outrage. Among those detained, according to organisers, were 11 Australians.

item.title: NSW’s first Migrant Workers Centre opens in Sydney
item.description: The New South Wales government has unveiled the state's first Migrant Workers Centre to support temporary visa holders. It means migrant workers in Sydney now have a dedicated hub to seek help over work exploitation. It’s backed by $6.5 million in state funding over the next four years to provide workplace, safety and immigration support to vulnerable migrants across the state.

item.title: New funding announced to fight deadly diphtheria outbreak in Australia
item.description: Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak in decades has spread across Northern Territory into Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with more than 230 [[as of May 21]] cases of the disease reported so far this year. Aboriginal Community Controlled Health Organisations and health workers are welcoming a $7 million federal government funding package aimed at supporting the response to the outbreak. But, experts say the re-emergence of the disease exposes serious gaps in the public health system.

item.title: Conservation protest takes aim at "needless" harvesting on Great Barrier Reef
item.description: Australia is home to the world's largest coral reef ecosystem, but conservationists say it's also the country's largest coral fishery. The sustainability of the Great Barrier Reef has been the subject of discussion among environmentalists for quite some time. Now, a group of conservationists have resorted to an unconventional method of putting the spotlight on the situation.

item.title: 'Come over': NZ minister invites Australians unhappy with CGT changes
item.description: Capital gains tax measures have also been part of New Zealand political debates.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: China’s revolving door of world leaders, DRC’s Ebola emergency & Trump’s ‘slush fund’
item.description: Beijing looks to capitalise on rival powers following back-to-back visits from the US and Russian presidents, as The Democratic Republic of the Congo struggles to contain a deadly Ebola outbreak. Plus, Donald Trump celebrates a landmark settlement with the IRS, and we investigate the art of the perfect diplomatic gift.

item.title: Jobless rate hits 4.5 year high as ASX posts strongest rally in weeks
item.description: Australia’s unemployment rate has climbed to its highest level since late 2021, with nearly 19,000 jobs lost and youth unemployment on the rise, raising fresh questions about the economic outlook and the path for interest rates. Deloitte Access Economics partner Stephen Smith breaks down what’s driving the shift and what it means in a climate of global uncertainty and elevated borrowing costs. Meanwhile, the sharemarket surged, with the ASX 200 posting its strongest gain in six weeks, lifted by materials, banks and real estate, as Heath Moss from HLM Investments unpacks what’s behind the rally and whether it can last.

item.title: Deported Australians on Gaza-bound flotilla allege abuse while detained by Israel
item.description: Israel's prison service has said all detainees were held "in accordance with the law, with full regard for their basic rights".

item.title: Deported Australians on Gaza-bound flotilla allege abuse while detained by Israel
item.description: Israel's prison service has said all detainees were held "in accordance with the law, with full regard for their basic rights".

item.title: Neo-Nazi organisation lodges legal challenge against hate group listing
item.description: Leader Thomas Sewell said the injunction seeks to stop the government from using "legislation to arrest or imprison" him.

item.title: 'They are very, very worried': Why Donald Trump's power could cut both ways
item.description: Trump-backed candidates are winning Republican primaries — but that could be a double-edged sword.

item.title: More Australians linked to IS leave Syria refugee camp | Morning News Bulletin 22 May 2026
item.description: Reports another group of Australians linked to the IS group have left a refugee camp in Syria; Australian activists detained by Israel set to be released; And in sport, a veteran AFL star prepares to make his 433rd appearance.

item.title: Democrats release long-withheld 'autopsy' on Kamala Harris' 2024 US election loss
item.description: The secrecy surrounding the report, which exposes divisions over why the party lost to Donald Trump, had angered some Democrats.

item.title: Fears for fragile ecosystems as brumby population explodes in iconic national park
item.description: Aerial shooting will resume next month, after newly revealed estimates suggested wild horse numbers had surged by thousands.

item.title: Australians released from Israeli detention tell of their experiences | Midday News Bulletin 22 May 2026
item.description: Australians released from Israeli detention tell of their experiences; Members of the Stolen Generations gather in Queensland to mark an anniversary; and in the NRL, the Dolphins make club history with a fourth-straight win.

item.title: Second IS-linked group attempting return faces 'same consequences', Plibersek says
item.description: It's believed the cohort will travel to Australia from Damascus after leaving Syria's al-Roj detention camp.

item.title: The online threat linked to Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak in decades
item.description: The disease had almost vanished. Experts say its resurgence reflects deeper problems.

item.title: SBS News in Easy English 22 May 2026
item.description: A daily 5 minute news wrap for English learners and people with disability

item.title: Ebola case confirmed in rebel-held area of DR Congo
item.description: Health workers are racing to contain the Bundibugyo strain of the virus in the Democratic Republic of Congo, for which there is currently no approved vaccine or treatment. The World Health Organisation says the Ebola outbreak spreading through the eastern D-R-C into neighbouring Uganda could be far larger than officially reported. In cities like Goma and Bunia, families are still trying to continue daily life amid growing fears the outbreak is spreading beyond control.

item.title: Aussie flotilla activists allege assault while in detention| Evening News Bulletin 22 May 2026
item.description: A Gaza flotilla activist alleges mistreatment by Israeli authorities; An Australian man dies while hiking the Inca Trail in Peru; Socceroos midfielder Matthew Leckie says competition is fierce for World Cup spots.

item.title: Australia's 'death tax' debate: What we really pay when we die
item.description: Labor says their trust taxation reforms close trust loopholes. The Opposition says they tax inherited wealth by stealth.

item.title: 'Has that been written off?': Fresh questions over Belle Gibson's $410,000 fine
item.description: The questions come after Premier Jacinta Allan said the consumer watchdog was "constantly and consistently" pursuing the fine.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS speaks to former Biden administration staffer Cory Alpert as primary elections get underway
item.description: United States President Donald Trump flexed his political muscle in this week's Republican primaries, reinforcing his iron grip on the party even as his approval ratings slide. But with anti-Trump sentiment expected to shape the November midterm elections, his dominance could prove to be either an asset — or a liability. SBS News' Josie Harvey spoke to former Biden White House staffer Cory Alpert about the dynamics of the GOP - and the Democrats - as the crucial midterm vote approaches.

item.title: 'Fake gas shortage': Premier's fracking comments draw fury from conservationists
item.description: The Conservation Council said WA was not facing a gas supply problem, but it did have a gas export problem.

item.title: Is space the next investment frontier? Elon Musk's SpaceX listing sparks investor buzz
item.description: The ASX finished the week higher as investors weighed strong gains in mining stocks against weakness in telcos, while attention also turned to the booming space economy. In this episode of SBS On the Money, Ricardo Gonçalves speaks with Pepperstone Head of Research Chris Weston about Elon Musk’s plans to list SpaceX at a reported valuation of US$1.75 trillion, what it means for future blockbuster IPOs including OpenAI and Anthropic, and whether space is becoming the next big investment opportunity. Chris Weston also breaks down the day on the Australian sharemarket and the outlook for interest rates.

item.title: 'Like a sneeze': Race to build Australian AI data centres generates concern
item.description: Tech companies are racing to build AI data centres, but there's a major question of where they should be built.

item.title: The two best places to invest your money in Australia after 'major' budget shake-up
item.description: Significant changes to tax discounts in the 2026 federal budget are likely to shift how and where people choose to invest.

item.title: Australia's slowdown, family fortunes — and the investment bet heading sky-high
item.description: The key money stories this week, broken down simply — and what they mean for you.

item.title: WHO says Ebola outbreak in DRC is spreading rapidly | Morning News Bulletin 23 May 2026
item.description: The World Health Organisation says an Ebola outbreak in the DRC is spreading rapidly; the prime minister pushes back on claims of death duties in the federal budget; Pep Guardiola to leave Man City.

item.title: Second-hand EV sales are surging - even classic cars are going electric
item.description: Australia’s electric vehicle (EV) market hit a new record in April. And sales of second-hand EVs are soaring too. Some owners are also converting classic cars.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS talks to Doctors Without Borders about the Ebola outbreak in the DRC
item.description: The World Health Organisation has sent a team of experts and supplies to the Democratic Republic of Congo to help combat the spread of Ebola. On Sunday, it declared the outbreak a public health emergency of international concern because of the high risk the disease could spread further beyond DR Congo's borders after two cases were confirmed in Kampala, the capital of neighbouring Uganda. Among the agencies assisting in the efforts is Medicins San Frontiers, also known as Doctors Without Borders. Simon Eccleshall is the Head of Programs for MSF in Australia, and he spoke with SBS World News reporter Alexandra Jones about the group's response.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as top US intelligence official
item.description: The first Hindu member of the White House and a former presidential candidate, Gabbard is known for her opposition to foreign wars.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as top US intelligence official
item.description: The first Hindu member of the White House and a former presidential candidate, Gabbard is known for her opposition to foreign wars.

item.title: INTERVIEW: SBS Nepali speaks to Bianca Adler after record breaking Mount Everest climb
item.description: A Melbourne teenager has made history, becoming the youngest Australian to reach the summit of Mount Everest.18-year-old Bianca Adler achieved the feat on her second attempt - completing the ascent of the nearly 9,000-metre [[8,849m]] summit with two Nepali guides. SBS Nepali executive producer Abhas Parajuli spoke to Bianca Adler about the challenges but also what it felt like being at the peak of the famous summit.

item.title: People with MS concerned about losing access to NDIS as survey reveals some already don't qualify for scheme
item.description: There are fears as the government seeks to achieve its budget savings with cutbacks to NDIS access, those who need support won't receive it. A new survey has found not all those with multiple sclerosis have access to the scheme - and for those who do, they face constant misunderstandings about their condition.

item.title: Inside the costly trend charging up Australia's classic car scene
item.description: A niche group of classic car owners are giving used vehicles an electric second life — but is it worth it?

item.title: Melbourne teenager becomes youngest Australian to summit Mount Everest
item.description: The 18-year-old's singular achievement comes as the number of climbers ascending to the world's tallest peak hits a record high.

item.title: Trump defends war against Iran and says he believes the conflict will be over soon | Midday News Bulletin 23 May 2026
item.description: Donald Trump defends war against Iran and says he believes the conflict will be over soon; Victorian Labor and Liberal conferences underway ahead of the state election; and in sport, Sydney FC take on Auckland in the A-League championship grand final.

item.title: SpaceX launches biggest model of Starship megarocket
item.description: SpaceX has launched its biggest Starship yet as the company races against Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin for space exploration dominance.

item.title: This economist predicted three World Cup winners. He's backed an underdog for 2026
item.description: The football fan has correctly predicted the World Cup winner since 2014. Here's the side he's picked as this year's champions.

item.title: Trump administration to end in-country applications for US permanent residency
item.description: It's just the latest step by the Trump administration to make legal immigration to the United States more difficult.

item.title: Politicians prepare for Victoria's state election | Evening News Bulletin 23 May 2026
item.description: Federal Labor and Liberal leaders visit Victoria ahead of its state election; three dead in a car crash in Sydney; more tributes for Jai Arrow after his diagnosis with MND.

item.title: Samuel's visa status made him easier to exploit. A new project hopes to help migrants like him
item.description: Temporary visa holders experiencing workplace issues in NSW have a new place to turn to for help.
